title: Heavy Metal Blood Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
al blood test for one type of metal, or you may have a test for a group of metals, called a "heavy metal panel."
Depending on what metals your health care provider is checking for, testing can also be done with samples of your urine, fingernails, or hair. But blood and urine are the samples that are most commonly used for heavy metal tests.
Other names: heavy metals panel, toxic metals, heavy metal toxicity test
What is it used for? 
Heavy metal testing is used to find out if you have been exposed to certain metals, and how much of the metal is in your system.
Why do I need a heavy metal blood test? 
Your provider may order a heavy metal blood test if you had a possible exposure to heavy metal or if you have symptoms of heavy metal poisoning.
Symptoms of heavy metal poisoning depend on the type of metal and how much exposure you had. Your symptoms may include:
Nausea and vomiting 
Abdominal (belly) pain 
Diarrhea 
Tingling in the hands and feet 
Shortness of breath 
Chills 
Muscle weakness 
Memory loss or changes in behavior 
Arrhythmia (problem with the rate or rhythm of your heartbeat 
If your job involves heavy metals, your employer may require regular testing to help monitor workplace safety.
Children under the age of 6 may need to be tested for lead poisoning if they have a risk of exposure to lead, even when they don't have symptoms. That's because very low levels of lead
